prompt_text: Baremetrics vs ChartMogul for early-stage SaaS founders and indie developers on Stripe
response:
For an early-stage SaaS or indie product running on **Stripe only**, pick based on what you care about most right now:  
- If you want **fast, simple visibility and built‑in dunning/cancel insights** with minimal setup, **Baremetrics** is usually the better fit.  
- If you want **free pricing at very low MRR and deeper analytics/segmentation for later**, **ChartMogul** is usually the better fit.

Below is the short, founder‑oriented breakdown.

---

## 1. How they differ for early‑stage & indie Stripe users

Both tools give you accurate **MRR, ARR, churn, LTV, cohorts** off of Stripe with minimal setup.[1][6]

Core differences that matter if you’re early and probably solo:

- **Complexity vs simplicity**
  - **Baremetrics:** Designed for **speed and simplicity**; “fast setup, simple SaaS metrics, and a founder‑friendly view of MRR, churn, and LTV.”[2]  
  - **ChartMogul:** Better for **analytics depth and board‑level clarity** with more charts, cohorts, and segmentation power.[2][3]

- **“All‑in‑one growth toolkit” vs pure analytics**
  - **Baremetrics:** Positions itself as an **all‑in‑one growth toolkit**: metrics + **revenue recovery (dunning), cancellation insights, and financial forecasting** in a single tool.[1][6]
  - **ChartMogul:** Focused on **subscription analytics and data control**; it does *not* include dunning or cancellation workflows, so you need separate tools to act on the insights.[3][6]

- **Data control & editing**
  - **ChartMogul:** Gives more **control over how metrics are calculated**, with detailed MRR movement tracking and data editing tools for “perfect accuracy.”[3]
  - **Baremetrics:** More opinionated and turnkey; less about custom data modeling, more about “just give me the right numbers now.”[2]

---

## 2. Pricing reality for early‑stage Stripe founders

For small, lean SaaS on Stripe, pricing and free tiers matter a lot.

- **Baremetrics**
  - Starts around **$49/mo (“Accelerator”, up to ~\$30K MRR)**, then scales with MRR.[1][7]
  - **No free tier** for production use.[1][7]
  - Revenue recovery (Recover) and Cancellation Insights are **paid add‑ons**.[1]

- **ChartMogul**
  - **Free up to \$10K MRR**, including core analytics and one CRM seat.[1][3][6]
  - Paid tiers (Starter / Launch / Scale) kick in after that; typical guidance: **free to \$10K MRR, then ≈\$129–200/mo** depending on plan.[1][4][7]
  - Pricing scales more with **ARR** than with MRR.[1]

Implication for you:

- If you’re **pre‑revenue → <\$10K MRR**, you can run **ChartMogul for free** and delay paying for analytics.[1][4][7]
- If paying $49/mo is acceptable and you *really* want built‑in dunning + cancel surveys in the same tool, **Baremetrics** makes sense even very early.[1][6]

---

## 3. Feature comparison (early‑stage‑relevant)

| Area | **Baremetrics** | **ChartMogul** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Core SaaS metrics (MRR/ARR/churn/LTV, cohorts) | **Yes** – full set[1][6] | **Yes** – full set[1][3][6] |
| Segmentation | Unlimited segments, unlimited dashboards[1] | Strong segmentation; dashboard caps on lower tiers[1][3] |
| **Dunning / failed payment recovery** | **Built‑in**: Recover (Stripe‑focused, customizable)[1][6] | **No** dunning – needs external tool[1][3][6] |
| **Cancellation surveys & follow‑ups** | **Included** (widget, reasons, follow‑up)[1][6] | Not included[1][6] |
| **Financial forecasting** | **P&L, runway, burn rate** via Forecast Plus with Xero/QuickBooks.[1][6] | Subscription revenue modeling only; no full finance forecast[1] |
| CRM | No native CRM; integrates with tools like HubSpot[1][6] | **Native CRM** on all plans, including free[1][3] |
| Free tier | **None** (paid from day one)[1][7] | **Free up to \$10K MRR**[1][3]
